metric_definitions.contact_canonical.n_present: Number of origins where security.txt was present (present=True).
metric_definitions.contact_canonical.n_present_has_canonica: Number of present security.txt files that contained at least one Canonical field.
metric_definitions.contact_canonical.n_present_has_contact: Number of present security.txt files that contained at least one Contact field.
metric_definitions.contact_canonical.n_present_multiple_con: Number of present security.txt files where the Contact field appeared more than once, i.e., after splitting the stored contacts string on commas there were multiple non-empty entries.
metric_definitions.contact_canonical.pct_present_with_canon: Percentage of present security.txt files that included at least one Canonical field.
metric_definitions.contact_canonical.pct_present_with_conta: Percentage of present security.txt files that included at least one Contact field.
metric_definitions.expires_stats.n_present: Number of origins where security.txt was present (present=True).
metric_definitions.expires_stats.n_present_expired_expires: Number of present security.txt files where has_expires=True but expires_valid=False, meaning the advertised Expires timestamp was already in the past.
metric_definitions.expires_stats.n_present_has_expires: Number of present security.txt files that contained a validly parsed Expires field.
metric_definitions.expires_stats.n_present_no_expires: Number of present security.txt files that did not include any Expires field.
metric_definitions.expires_stats.n_present_valid_expires: Number of present security.txt files where has_expires=True and expires_valid=True, i.e., the Expires timestamp was still in the future at scan time.
metric_definitions.expires_stats.pct_present_with_expires: Percentage of present security.txt files that included an Expires field (n_present_has_expires / n_present).
metric_definitions.expires_stats.pct_valid_expires_among_ex: Percentage of security.txt files with an Expires field where the Expires value was still valid at scan time (n_present_valid_expires / n_present_has_expires).
metric_definitions.field_combinations.contact_and_expires: Number of origins with present=True where security.txt contained both at least one Contact field and a valid Expires field.
metric_definitions.field_combinations.contact_only: Number of origins with present=True where security.txt contained at least one Contact field but no valid Expires field.
metric_definitions.field_combinations.expires_only: Number of origins with present=True where security.txt contained a valid Expires field but no Contact field.
metric_definitions.field_combinations.present_without_conta: Number of origins with present=True but with neither Contact nor Expires fields detected. This should be zero in normal RFC 9116 files and is included as a sanity check.
metric_definitions.location_counts: Histogram of locations where security.txt was successfully found among origins with present=True, typically '/.well-known/security.txt' and '/security.txt'.
metric_definitions.securitytxt_overview.n_missing: Number of origins that were probed (not origin offline) but did not yield any usable security.txt file (present=False).
metric_definitions.securitytxt_overview.n_origin_offline_se: Number of origins where the securitytxt module recorded error='origin offline', indicating that the origin was treated as offline and security.txt was not probed.
metric_definitions.securitytxt_overview.n_origins: Number of origins that appeared in the securitytxt module results.
metric_definitions.securitytxt_overview.n_other_errors: Number of origins where the securitytxt module recorded an error that was neither 'origin offline' nor a timeout, such as file_too_large or too_many_lines.
metric_definitions.securitytxt_overview.n_present: Number of origins where present=True, meaning at least one usable security.txt file was found at /.well-known/security.txt or /security.txt and contained a Contact or Expires field.
metric_definitions.securitytxt_overview.n_timeouts: Number of origins where the securitytxt module recorded an error starting with 'timeout:', indicating that the HTTPS request for security.txt did not complete before the timeout.
